BriefRankingEntry.description: One line of the candidate ranking, as it appears on the results screen.
BriefRankingEntry.title: BriefRankingEntry

DemoSeedResponse.description: The seeded project and its completed screening run.
DemoSeedResponse.title: DemoSeedResponse

DocumentProcessingStatus.description:
Spec §15.6 — lifecycle of an uploaded permitting document.

CONTRACT CHANGE (integration). v1 drafted this as four members
(``uploaded``/``processing``/``completed``/``failed``), but the
document-analysis workflow needs to report which LangGraph stage a document
is in, and it carried a second, parallel ``DocumentWorkflowStatus`` enum over
the same column to say so. Two enums for one column is exactly the drift the
contract exists to prevent, so the workflow's members are folded in here and
the duplicate is gone. ``PROCESSING`` is retained as the coarse state for a
document that is in flight but not in a named stage.
DocumentProcessingStatus.title: DocumentProcessingStatus

DocumentWorkflowEventResponse.description:
One LangGraph node's public audit record.

Named for the table it reads (``document_workflow_events``) so it is not
confused with ``WorkflowEventRead``, which is a screening run's step. Carries
the step, tool, status, structured output, and errors — never model reasoning
(CLAUDE.md rule 6).
DocumentWorkflowEventResponse.title: DocumentWorkflowEventResponse

FindingCategory.description:
Which screening dimension a finding belongs to.

The first four mirror the four scoring categories of spec §14.
``DATA_COMPLETENESS`` carries known-unknowns that carry no points.
FindingCategory.title: FindingCategory

FindingGroup.description:
How a finding should be presented (spec §10.4).

Missing information is kept separate from confirmed risks: an absent input is
not evidence of a problem, and the two must never be conflated in the UI.

``REQUIREMENT`` was added at integration for document-derived permitting
requirements. Filing them under ``RISK`` would assert that "a conditional-use
permit is required" is a problem with the site, which it is not — it is an
obligation, and it gets its own list on the site page.
FindingGroup.title: FindingGroup

FindingSeverity.description: Spec §13. A single ``FATAL`` finding forces a ``REJECT`` recommendation.
FindingSeverity.title: FindingSeverity

FindingSourceType.description: Spec §15.5 — how a finding was produced.
FindingSourceType.title: FindingSourceType

PermittingAnalysisStatus.description:
Whether the permitting category rests on a real document.

Deterministic screening never analyzes a document, so a run on this branch
always reports ``NOT_ANALYZED``. ``ANALYZED`` is reserved for the
document-analysis branch, which owns the only code allowed to set it.
PermittingAnalysisStatus.title: PermittingAnalysisStatus

ProjectDashboardItem.description:
A project as the dashboard lists it.

The rollups are computed by the backend from the latest completed run. The
dashboard, the results table, and the site page must agree on them, and they
only can if one place derives them (spec §9.5).
ProjectDashboardItem.title: ProjectDashboardItem

ProjectStatus.description: Serialized as a plain string, which is all the v1 contract promises.
ProjectStatus.title: ProjectStatus

ProjectType.description: Spec §10.2. ``COMMUNITY_SOLAR`` comes from the §16.1 request example.
ProjectType.title: ProjectType

RecommendationStatus.description: Spec §8.1D / §14 — assigned deterministically from score and findings.
RecommendationStatus.title: RecommendationStatus

RequirementCategory.description:
The kind of permitting obligation a document-derived finding states.

Distinct from ``FindingCategory``, which says which of the four scoring
dimensions a finding belongs to. A document finding is always
``FindingCategory.PERMITTING``; this says what sort of permitting item it is.
RequirementCategory.title: RequirementCategory

ReviewDecision.description: A reviewer's action on a finding. Maps onto ``ReviewStatus``.
ReviewDecision.title: ReviewDecision

ReviewStatus.description: State of a finding in the human-review loop (spec §8.1F).
ReviewStatus.title: ReviewStatus

RiskFindingRead.description: A persisted finding as returned by the API, whatever produced it.
RiskFindingRead.title: RiskFindingRead

RowValidationError.description:
One reason one CSV row was not imported.

``row_number`` is the line number in the uploaded file, counting the header
as line 1, so it matches what the user sees in a spreadsheet.
RowValidationError.title: RowValidationError

ScoreBreakdownItem.description: One scoring rule, and what it awarded. Spec §9.5 — show every deduction.
ScoreBreakdownItem.title: ScoreBreakdownItem

ScreeningCriteria.description:
Thresholds a candidate site is screened against (spec §10.2 section B).

``minimum_acres`` is not repeated here — it lives on the project itself, as
in the contract's worked example.
ScreeningCriteria.title: ScreeningCriteria

ScreeningRunDetail.description:
A run plus its ranked results, highest score first.

The project is embedded because every screen that shows a ranking also shows
the criteria the ranking was produced against; sending them together means the
UI cannot render a score next to a stale threshold.
ScreeningRunDetail.title: ScreeningRunDetail

ScreeningRunStatus.description: Spec §18 — partial completion is a first-class outcome.
ScreeningRunStatus.title: ScreeningRunStatus

SiteBriefRead.description: Everything the printable brief renders, in the order spec §8.1G lists it.
SiteBriefRead.title: SiteBriefRead

SiteDetail.description:
One site with its most recent screening result, if it has been screened.

``score`` is ``None`` until a run has scored the site — an unscreened site is
a normal state, not an error.

``permitting_requirements`` are the document-derived findings for the site,
each carrying its evidence and its review status. They are listed separately
from ``risks`` because a permitting obligation is not a defect in the site,
and from ``missing_information`` because it is not an unknown.
SiteDetail.title: SiteDetail

SiteImportResult.description: The response to a CSV import. Rows that fail validation are never persisted.
SiteImportResult.title: SiteImportResult

SiteScreeningResult.description:
One ranked site, with everything needed to explain its result.

The counts and the next action are computed here rather than in the UI. They
are conclusions drawn from findings and a recommendation status, and the
backend is the only place allowed to draw them (CLAUDE.md, spec §9.1).
SiteScreeningResult.title: SiteScreeningResult

info.description: Evidence-backed site triage for renewable energy and power infrastructure projects. Foundation build — endpoints are added by feature agents (see docs/PARALLEL_TASKS.md).
info.title: SiteSift API

post.description:
Load the sample solar project: one project, five sites, one completed run.

Safe to call repeatedly. A second call returns the existing demo — 200, not
201 — and creates nothing.

patch.description:
Approve, edit, reject, or escalate a finding.

Append-only: each decision writes a new ``reviews`` row and the extracted
original is preserved, so an edit never destroys what the document actually
said (spec §9.3).

get.description: Newest first — the dashboard shows the most recent work at the top.

schema.title: Response List Projects Api Projects Get

get.description:
Projects with their candidate count, top score, and risk rollups.

Declared before ``/{project_id}`` so that "dashboard" is not parsed as a
project id.

schema.title: Response List Project Dashboard Api Projects Dashboard Get

post.description:
Screen every candidate site in the project.

Spec §18: a repeated request carrying the same ``Idempotency-Key`` returns
the run that already exists — 200, not 201 — rather than screening twice.

get.description:
The project's most recent run, with its ranked results.

The results screen needs the project, the run, and the ranking together. 404
means the project has not been screened yet — a normal state the UI shows as
an empty state, not an error.
In import order, which is the order the user gave us.

schema.title: Response List Sites Api Projects  Project Id  Sites Get

post.description:
Import candidate sites from a CSV.

Rows that fail validation are reported and not persisted; rows already
imported are reported as duplicates and not persisted twice. The valid rows
are written in one transaction, so a failure leaves the project as it was.

get.description: The audit trail for a run, in the order the steps ran (spec §18).

schema.title: Response List Screening Events Api Screenings  Screening Id  Events Get

get.description: A site with its latest score, positive signals, risks, and missing information.
Every permitting document uploaded for a site, oldest first.

schema.title: Response List Site Documents Api Sites  Site Id  Documents Get

post.description: Upload and validate one zoning/permitting PDF for a candidate site.

get.description:
Every finding for a site — deterministic and document-derived alike.

Evidence is eager-loaded: a document-derived claim is only meaningful with the
excerpt it rests on, and the UI must never show one without the other.

schema.title: Response List Site Findings Api Sites  Site Id  Findings Get

get.description:
Report service liveness and whether the database is reachable.

A missing database does not fail the probe: the API is still up, and the
frontend shell needs to render either way.
